prerequisites: A local filesystem path where the SQLite database file will be created or written (the destination_path config field). SQLite stores the entire database in this single ordinary disk file.
Write access to the directory containing destination_path so the connector can create and update the database file. SQLite has no server process, so no host, port, or network connection is required.
No login account, API key, or other credentials are required. SQLite is serverless and zero-configuration, with no separate server to install, configure, or authenticate against.

authMethods.name: No authentication
authMethods.summary: SQLite is a serverless, file-based database with no authentication layer. The connector declares no secret fields, so no credentials are configured. Access is governed by operating-system file permissions on the database file at destination_path rather than by any database-level username, password, or token.

setupSteps.title: Choose the destination database file path
setupSteps.body: Decide on the local filesystem location for the SQLite database file and set it as the destination_path config field. SQLite keeps the complete database (tables, indexes, and data) in this one file. If the file does not exist it will be created; if it exists, the connector writes into it.

setupSteps.title: Ensure filesystem write permissions
setupSteps.body: Confirm the process running the connector has permission to create and write the file at destination_path and to its parent directory. Because SQLite is zero-configuration and has no server process, there is nothing else to start or configure beyond a writable path.

setupSteps.title: Run the sync
setupSteps.body: With destination_path set, run the connector. It opens or creates the SQLite file at that path and writes the synced records into it. No connection string, server address, or credentials are involved.
